text: Lesson 25
zh: 第25课

text: Non-auditory effects of noise
zh: 噪音的非听觉效应

text: What conclusion does the author draw about noise and health in this piece?
zh: 作者在这篇文章中对噪音与健康之间的关系得出了什么结论？

text: Many people in industry and the Services,
zh: 工业界和军队中的许多人，

text: who have practical experience of noise,
zh: 他们对噪音有实际的了解，

text: regard any investigation of this question as a waste of time;
zh: 认为对这个问题进行任何调查都是浪费时间；

text: they are not prepared even to admit the possibility that noise affects people.
zh: 他们甚至不愿承认噪音会影响人的可能性。

text: On the other hand,
zh: 另一方面，

text: those who dislike noise will sometimes use most inadequate evidence
zh: 那些讨厌噪音的人有时会用极不充分的证据

text: to support their pleas for a quieter society.
zh: 来支持他们呼吁建立一个更安静社会的主张。

text: This is a pity, because noise abatement really is a good cause,
zh: 这很遗憾，因为减少噪音本是一项正当的事业，

text: and it is likely to be discredited if it gets to be associated with bad science.
zh: 如果它与糟糕的科学扯上关系，很可能会失去信誉。

text: One allegation often made is that noise produces mental illness.
zh: 人们常提出的一种说法是，噪音会导致精神疾病。

text: A recent article in a weekly newspaper, for instance,
zh: 例如，最近一家周报上的一篇文章，

text: was headed with a striking illustration of a lady in a state of considerable distress,
zh: 配了一幅引人注目的插图，画的是一位极度痛苦的女士，

text: with the caption 'She was yet another victim, reduced to a screaming wreck'.
zh: 配文写道：“她是又一个受害者，被折磨得只剩下尖叫。”

text: On turning eagerly to the text, one learns that the lady was a typist
zh: 当人们急切地翻开正文一看，才知道这位女士是一名打字员，

text: who found the sound of office typewriters worried her more and more
zh: 她发现办公室打字机的声音让她越来越心烦，

text: until eventually she had to go into a mental hospital.
zh: 最终不得不住进精神病院。

text: Now the snag in this sort of anecdote is of course that one cannot distinguish cause and effect.
zh: 当然，这类轶事的问题在于，人们无法区分因果关系。

text: Was the noise a cause of the illness,
zh: 究竟是噪音导致了疾病，

text: or were the complaints about noise merely a symptom?
zh: 还是对噪音的抱怨只是症状之一？

text: Another patient might equally well complain
zh: 另一位病人同样可能会抱怨说，

text: that her neighbours were combining to slander her and persecute her,
zh: 她的邻居联合起来诽谤她、迫害她，

text: and yet one might be cautious about believing this statement.
zh: 但人们对这种说法恐怕会持谨慎态度。

text: What is needed in the case of noise is a study of large numbers of people living under noisy conditions,
zh: 就噪音而言，需要的是对生活在嘈杂环境中的大量人群进行研究，

text: to discover whether they are mentally ill more often than other people are.
zh: 以了解他们是否比其他人更容易患精神疾病。

text: Some time ago the United States Navy, for instance,
zh: 例如，不久前，美国海军

text: examined a very large number of men working on aircraft carriers:
zh: 对在航空母舰上工作的大量人员进行了调查：

text: the study was known as Project Anehin.
zh: 这项研究被称为“阿尼欣计划”。

text: It can be unpleasant to live even several miles from an aerodrome;
zh: 即使住在离机场几英里外的地方，日子也可能很难过；

text: if you think what it must be like to share the deck of a ship with several squadrons of jet aircraft,
zh: 如果你想象一下与几个中队的喷气式飞机共处同一层甲板会是什么感觉，

text: you will realize that a modern navy is a good place to study noise.
zh: 你就会明白，现代海军是研究噪音的好地方。

text: But neither psychiatric interviews nor objective tests
zh: 但无论是精神科访谈还是客观测试，

text: were able to show any effects upon these American sailors.
zh: 都未能显示出这些美国水兵受到任何影响。

text: This result merely confirms earlier American and British studies:
zh: 这一结果不过是印证了早先美国和英国的研究：

text: if there is any effect of noise upon mental health,
zh: 如果噪音对心理健康确实有影响，

text: it must be so small that present methods of psychiatric diagnosis cannot find it.
zh: 那也一定微小到目前的精神病诊断方法无法察觉的程度。

text: That does not prove that it does not exist; but it does mean
zh: 这并不能证明这种影响不存在；但这确实意味着，

text: that noise is less dangerous than, say being brought up in an orphanage
zh: 噪音的危害程度比不上，比如说，在孤儿院长大——

text: --which really is a mental health hazard.
zh: 那才是真正危害心理健康的因素。
